nodes.label: Aspire Orchestration Skill (.opencode copy)
nodes.description: Skill for orchestrating distributed .NET applications via Aspire CLI. Covers AppHost management, resource lifecycle, integration packages, MCP tools, observability, and diagnostics.

nodes.label: Aspire CLI
nodes.description: Command-line tool for running, debugging, and managing Aspire distributed applications. Primary interface for developers interacting with the AppHost.

nodes.label: AppHost
nodes.description: Central project defining the resource graph of an Aspire distributed application. Resources include .NET projects, containers, databases, message brokers, etc.

nodes.label: Aspire Resource
nodes.description: A component in the Aspire resource graph (e.g. .NET project, container, database, cache). Each resource can be started, stopped, restarted, and rebuilt individually.

nodes.label: Aspire Integration
nodes.description: NuGet package that adds service capabilities to an AppHost (e.g. PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ). Added via 'aspire add' and documented at aspire.dev.

nodes.label: aspire start
nodes.description: Starts the AppHost and all resources. Automatically stops previous instance. Supports --isolated for worktree environments.

nodes.label: aspire stop
nodes.description: Stops the running AppHost and all its resources.

nodes.label: aspire describe
nodes.description: Lists all resources with their status, endpoints, health, and available commands. Supports --format Json for machine-readable output.

nodes.label: aspire resource <name> <command>
nodes.description: Runs a command on a specific resource: start, stop, restart, rebuild. 'rebuild' rebuilds and restarts only that .NET project resource.

nodes.label: aspire logs
nodes.description: Views console output from resources. Accepts an optional resource name filter.

nodes.label: aspire otel
nodes.description: OpenTelemetry subcommands: 'aspire otel logs [resource]' for structured logs, 'aspire otel traces [resource]' for distributed traces, 'aspire otel logs --trace-id <id>' for trace-scoped logs.

nodes.label: aspire add
nodes.description: Adds an integration NuGet package to the AppHost project interactively.

nodes.label: aspire docs
nodes.description: Subcommands: search (full-text search of aspire.dev), get (retrieve page by slug), list (browse available pages).

nodes.label: aspire doctor
nodes.description: Environment diagnostics tool that validates prerequisites (Docker, .NET SDK, etc.) before running AppHost.

nodes.label: aspire mcp
nodes.description: Lists and calls MCP tools exposed by resources (e.g. WithPostgresMcp for SQL queries). Supports --input for JSON-encoded parameters.

nodes.label: aspire wait
nodes.description: Blocks execution until a specific resource reports as healthy. Used in scripts after 'aspire start'.

nodes.label: Isolated Mode
nodes.description: Flag --isolated for 'aspire start' to avoid port conflicts and state sharing when running in git worktrees.

nodes.label: Resource MCP Tools
nodes.description: Expose domain-specific tooling via MCP protocol (e.g. WithPostgresMcp adds SQL query tools). Discoverable via 'aspire mcp tools'.

nodes.label: Aspire Observability
nodes.description: Structured logging, distributed tracing, and console output for resources via OpenTelemetry. Queried with 'aspire otel' subcommands.

nodes.label: Aspire Documentation
nodes.description: Official documentation at aspire.dev and learn.microsoft.com/microsoft/aspire. Accessed via 'aspire docs search/get/list' CLI commands.

nodes.label: Aspire Debug Workflow
nodes.description: Systematic debugging process: 1) aspire describe for status, 2) aspire otel logs for structured logs, 3) aspire logs for console output, 4) aspire otel traces for distributed traces.

nodes.label: Aspire Code Change Workflow
nodes.description: Full restart (aspire start) for AppHost changes; per-resource rebuild (aspire resource <name> rebuild) for .NET project changes. Interpreted resources may auto-reload.

nodes.label: Playwright CLI Skill (.opencode copy)
nodes.description: Skill for browser automation, web testing, and test generation using the playwright-cli tool. Covers core commands, sessions, snapshots, mocking, tracing, and spec-driven testing.

nodes.label: playwright-cli
nodes.description: CLI tool for browser automation. Supports navigation, interaction, snapshots, storage management, request mocking, tracing, video recording, and test generation via TypeScript code output.

nodes.label: Core Browser Commands
nodes.description: open, goto, click, dblclick, type, fill, drag, drop, hover, select, upload, check, uncheck, snapshot, eval, dialog-accept/dismiss, resize, close.

nodes.label: Accessibility Snapshot
nodes.description: DOM accessibility tree snapshot with element references (e.g. e1, e2). Supports depth limiting, element-scoping, and bounding box inclusion. Default interaction target for playwright-cli.

nodes.label: Element References
nodes.description: Snapshot-generated element identifiers (e.g. e15) used for interaction. Also supports CSS selectors, Playwright locators (getByRole, getByTestId), and direct eval for attribute inspection.

nodes.label: Browser Sessions
nodes.description: Named, isolated browser contexts with independent cookies, storage, cache, history, and tabs. Managed via -s flag, list/close/close-all/kill-all commands, and persistent profiles.

nodes.label: Storage State Management
nodes.description: Save/restore cookies, localStorage, and sessionStorage to/from JSON files. Supports individual cookie CRUD, localStorage CRUD, sessionStorage CRUD, and IndexedDB inspection.

nodes.label: Request Mocking
nodes.description: Intercept and mock network requests via route/unroute commands. Supports custom status, JSON body, headers, URL patterns (glob), and advanced scenarios via run-code.

nodes.label: Execution Tracing
nodes.description: Start/stop trace recording capturing actions, DOM snapshots, screenshots, network activity, console logs, and timing. Outputs .trace, .network, and resources/ files.

nodes.label: Test Generation
nodes.description: Every playwright-cli action generates corresponding Playwright TypeScript code. Code can be copied into test files. Supports semantic locators (role-based) and manual assertion addition.

nodes.label: Spec-Driven Testing
nodes.description: End-to-end workflow: plan (explore + spec file), generate (walk spec steps with CLI → emit test code), heal (debug failures, fix locators, reconcile spec). Uses seed tests and debug mode.

nodes.label: Playwright Test Runner
nodes.description: npx playwright test command for running tests. Supports --debug=cli for interactive debugging with playwright-cli attach. PLAYWRIGHT_HTML_OPEN=never suppresses HTML report.

nodes.label: Debug Mode (--debug=cli)
nodes.description: Pauses test at start and prints debugging instructions with a session name (tw-XXXX). playwright-cli attach tw-XXXX connects the CLI to the paused test page for interactive exploration.

nodes.label: Custom Code Execution (run-code)
nodes.description: Executes arbitrary Playwright JavaScript for advanced scenarios: geolocation, permissions, media emulation, wait strategies, iframes, file downloads, clipboard, page info, error handling.

nodes.label: Video Recording
nodes.description: Start/stop video capture (.webm) with chapter markers, descriptions, and duration controls. Referenced in SKILL.md but separate reference file.

nodes.label: Element Attributes Reference
nodes.description: Guide for inspecting element id, class, data-* attributes, computed styles, and DOM properties using eval() when snapshot does not reveal them.

nodes.label: Playwright Tests Reference
nodes.description: Guide for running Playwright tests with npx playwright test and debugging with --debug=cli mode using playwright-cli attach for interactive sessions.

nodes.label: Request Mocking Reference
nodes.description: Advanced request mocking patterns: conditional responses, real response modification, network failure simulation, delayed responses, URL glob patterns.

nodes.label: Running Code Reference
nodes.description: Comprehensive guide for playwright-cli run-code: geolocation, permissions, media emulation, wait strategies, frames/iframes, file downloads, clipboard, page info, JS execution, error handling, complex workflows.

nodes.label: Session Management Reference
nodes.description: Named browser sessions, isolation properties, attach/detach (CDP, extension, channel), persistent profiles, configuration, concurrent scraping, A/B testing, best practices.

nodes.label: Spec-Driven Testing Reference
nodes.description: Three-phase workflow: planning (explore app, write spec file), generation (walk spec steps, emit TypeScript tests), healing (debug failures, fix locators, reconcile spec).

nodes.label: Storage State Reference
nodes.description: Complete storage management: save/restore full state, cookie CRUD with options, localStorage CRUD, sessionStorage CRUD, IndexedDB operations, authentication state reuse patterns.

nodes.label: Test Generation Reference
nodes.description: How playwright-cli actions generate Playwright TypeScript code. Semantic locators, assertion patterns (toHaveText, toBeVisible, toMatchAriaSnapshot), generate-locator command, best practices.

nodes.label: Tracing Reference
nodes.description: Execution trace capture with .trace (action log), .network (HTTP log), resources/ directory. Use cases: debugging failed actions, analyzing performance, capturing evidence. Comparison with video/screenshot.

nodes.label: Raw Output Mode (--raw)
nodes.description: Global flag stripping page status and snapshot sections from output, returning only the result value. Used for piping into other tools (jq, file redirection).

nodes.label: Tab Management
nodes.description: Commands for multi-tab workflows: tab-list, tab-new, tab-close, tab-select. Supports index-based tab switching.

nodes.label: Keyboard and Mouse Commands
nodes.description: Keyboard: press, keydown, keyup. Mouse: mousemove, mousedown, mouseup, mousewheel. Supports modifier keys and right-click.

nodes.label: Navigation Commands
nodes.description: go-back, go-forward, reload for browser history navigation.

nodes.label: Save As Commands
nodes.description: screenshot (page or element, with filename option) and pdf generation.

nodes.label: DevTools Commands
nodes.description: console (view console messages), requests (list network requests), request (inspect single request), run-code (advanced scripting), tracing, video, show --annotate (UI review), generate-locator, highlight.

nodes.label: playwright-cli Installation
nodes.description: Global install via npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest. Local fallback via npx --no-install playwright-cli.

nodes.label: Browser Configuration
nodes.description: Browser selection (chrome, firefox, webkit, msedge), persistent/in-memory profiles, CDP attach, Playwright Extension attach, config file support, headed mode.

nodes.label: Seed Test
nodes.description: Minimal Playwright test (navigation/login) that lands the page in the initial state for all spec scenarios. Foundation of the plan-generate-heal workflow.

nodes.label: Spec File
nodes.description: Markdown file (specs/<feature>.plan.md) containing application overview, grouped test scenarios with numbered steps and expect bullets. Each scenario maps to one test file.

nodes.label: Heal Workflow
nodes.description: Diagnose failing tests via debug mode, fix locator/assertion drift, reconcile spec with app behavior. Never use sleeps/networkidle; use test.fixme for confirmed bugs.

edges.label: Aspire skill references Playwright CLI for functional testing
AppHost defines resources in the resource graph
Start command is part of Aspire CLI
Stop command is part of Aspire CLI
Describe command is part of Aspire CLI
Resource command is part of Aspire CLI
OTEL subcommands display structured observability data
MCP subcommands list and call resource MCP tools
Wait command blocks until resource is healthy
--isolated flag prevents port conflicts in worktrees
Observability and MCP tools are both resource-level capabilities
Debug workflow starts with describe (status check)
Debug workflow uses structured logs and traces
Document describes keyboard and mouse commands
Session management reference documents named sessions and isolation
Session management reference documents browser configuration
Request mocking reference uses run-code for advanced scenarios
Storage state reference uses run-code for advanced operations
Spec-driven testing uses test generation for automatic code emission
Spec-driven testing uses the Playwright test runner
Spec-driven testing uses debug mode for interactive sessions
Spec-driven testing defines and uses seed tests
Spec-driven testing produces and consumes spec files
Spec-driven testing includes healing workflow for failures
Spec-driven testing references request mocking during exploration
Test generation uses element references for locator creation
Test generation uses element attributes for stable locators
Tests reference documents the test runner usage
Generated tests depend on Playwright test runner
Spec-driven testing depends on test generation
Healing depends on debug mode for diagnosis
Healing may use traces for diagnosis
Healing may use request mocking to isolate failures
Session management covers tab lifecycle within sessions
Tracing reference compares traces vs video vs screenshots
Storage state is managed per browser session
Debugging generates code that can be used as test generation output
Snapshots provide element references for interaction
Element attributes reference extends element refs with attribute inspection
Raw output mode strips snapshot sections for piping
Raw output mode strips snapshot sections when inspecting attributes
Video recording is an alternative/complement to tracing
Debug mode is documented in the tests reference
